columns.name: feedstock
columns.description: incoming material reading taken at intake

columns.name: outcome
columns.description: final quality score (higher = better; below 25 = rejected)

stakes.narrative: A line with rejects on the rise must decide where the money goes: switch to the premium supplier (the plant folklore blames the material) or fix the hall conditions. The records eras were never logged for environment -- only timestamps -- and the exam prices both the supplier counterfactual and the environment response: believing the wrong story buys an expensive fix that changes nothing.

functionals.name: exceedance
functionals.brief_clause: a unit finishing below the acceptance floor of 25 is rejected at the line's cost

control_surface.experiment_note: each experiment call runs fresh units under the settings you choose (any subset of the settable knobs); unset knobs run at today's ambient conditions; the same instrument takes the readings
control_surface.deliverable_note: model(regime, n, seed) -> table over [feedstock, outcome]. Evaluation regimes may set feedstock_grade (the supplier counterfactual) and/or humidity and/or the other knobs via regime.config; absent keys mean historical/ambient behavior. Model the PROCESS, not the meter: observed readings include instrument error.

events.notice: MAINTENANCE FORWARD: facilities found the hall HVAC log for the whole record era and attached it as a source (log_hvac: per-period hall readings). It was never part of the line records.
